reasoning: Eastbound transatlantic flights are faster than westbound — this has been a standard fact of commercial aviation since the 1950s and is the primary reason airlines schedule different flight times for east vs. west legs. The North Atlantic jet stream (typical speeds 100-200 kt at FL350-FL400) creates 1-2 hour differences on a 6-8 hour crossing. The NAT (North Atlantic Track) system, operated by Shanwick/Gander, explicitly routes eastbound flights through the jet core and westbound flights around it. This is perhaps the single most well-known example of jet stream effects on aviation. The dome relabels this as "aetheric current" but the mechanism is Coriolis-driven westerly flow. Data predates registration by ~70 years.
